scenario: Huge number of negative dentries cause massive calls to shrink_slab() --> prune_super() --> prune_dcache_sb() --> __prune_dcache_sb() and soft lockups, rcu_sched CPU stalls, and a hard lockup
symptoms: Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP
NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 8

workflow.thought: Check kernel log for lockup messages to confirm the crash cause
workflow.observation: Hard LOCKUP messages indicating watchdog detection

workflow.thought: Examine soft lockup patterns to identify spinlock contention
Get backtrace of all tasks to see which spinlocks are contended
workflow.action: bt -a | grep "exception RIP: native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath" -A3 | awk '$5~/RDI:/{print $6}' | sort | uniq -c
workflow.observation: Count of tasks stuck on different spinlock addresses

workflow.thought: Disassemble rcu_check_callbacks to identify spinlock address
Check what the spinlock at address ffffffff9b67a980 is
Examine the spinlock state to understand contention
workflow.observation: Spinlock is locked, pending, and contended

workflow.thought: Analyze the lock value to understand its state
workflow.observation: Binary representation showing lock bits set

workflow.thought: Check CPU 4's stack to understand rcu_sched stall processing
Identify the rcu_state structure for rcu_sched
Examine the rcu_node structure within rcu_sched_state
workflow.observation: Address of first rcu_node in rcu_sched_state

workflow.thought: Check the list of rcu_state flavors
List rcu_state flavors to see what's being processed
workflow.observation: List of rcu_state structures including rcu_sched

workflow.thought: Examine memory content to see what's being written
workflow.observation: Text showing rcu_sched stall messages being written

workflow.thought: Disassemble __prune_dcache_sb to identify dcache_lru_lock
Identify the symbol for the spinlock address
Examine the state of dcache_lru_lock spinlock
Analyze the lock value to understand contention level
Check MCS spinlock nodes for dcache_lru_lock waiters
workflow.action: bt -a | grep "exception RIP: native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath" -A4 | grep "RDI: ffffffff9b608c40" -A1 | awk '$3~/R8:/{print $4}' > nodes.txt
workflow.observation: Extract MCS node addresses to file for analysis

workflow.thought: Disassemble native_queued_spin_lock_slowpath to understand wait loop
workflow.observation: Instruction in wait loop for MCS spinlock

workflow.thought: Examine MCS spinlock structure for a waiting CPU
workflow.observation: MCS spinlock showing next pointer and locked state

workflow.thought: Check overall kernel memory usage to identify slab bloat
workflow.observation: SLAB using 79% of total memory (789 GB)

workflow.thought: List slab caches sorted by size to identify largest consumers
workflow.action: kmem -s | awk 'NR>1{printf($1 "\t" $NF "\t %8.3f MiB\n", $2*$4/2**20)}' | sort -k3nr | column -t | head

workflow.thought: Check dentry cache specifically for size details
workflow.action: kmem -s dentry | awk 'NR>1{printf($1 "\t" $NF "\t %8.3f GiB\n", $2*$4/2**30)}' | sort -k3nr | column -t | head

workflow.thought: Examine dentry statistics to see negative dentry count
workflow.observation: nr_negative = 4281697422, showing huge negative dentry count

workflow.thought: Check negative_dentry_limit value to see if it's set

root_cause_analysis: Lots of CPUs were getting busy with slab shrinking for page reclaiming. The dentry slab cache was quite largely bloated due to a huge number of negative dentries at that time.
Soft lockups happened on lots of CPUs that were getting stuck waiting on dcache_lru_lock spinlock before carrying out slab shrinking for page reclaiming.
This caused the rcu_sched CPU stalls detected on multiple CPUs in the subsequent APIC timer interrupt contexts.

Since it's quite time consuming to write a huge amount of rcu_sched messages and call traces to the console and also it has to be done with the rnp->lock spinlock being held, the rnp->lock spinlock was severely contended and caused the CPU to invoke the hard lockup and the crash in the end.
